title: Epic
description: Delivery grouping inside a venture (Opportunity Management v2 delivery spine: Team/Project -> Epic -> Use-Case). An epic lives in a backlog (backlogRef, N:1; the team is reached indirectly via the backlog), links up to strategic initiatives (initiativeRefs[], M:N) and down to the use-cases it groups (useCaseRefs[]). storyPoints is derived (Sigma of referenced use-cases); it may be omitted (computed on read) or stored as a cached rollup (#015 open question 3).

tenantId.description: Reserved for the future multi-tenant SaaS layer; null in the local file-based store.

workspaceId.description: Reserved workspace scope for the future SaaS layer; null in the local store.

slug.description: Filesystem-friendly slug, also the '<slug>.epic.json' filename stem.

backlogRef.description: Id of the backlog (backlog.schema.json) this epic lives in (N:1). The team is reached indirectly (epic -> backlog -> team).

initiativeRefs.description: Ids of the strategic initiatives (initiative.schema.json) this epic serves (M:N). Points volatile -> stable.

useCaseRefs.description: Ids of the use-cases (use-case.schema.json) grouped by this epic.

storyPoints.description: Optional cached rollup = Sigma of the referenced use-cases' storyPoints. Omit or null to compute on read.
